Creation Date: 10/03/2019 --> Boopie Miller Scores 25 and No. 11 Seed SMU Beats 14th-seeded Syracuse 86-69 in the ACC Tournament - Atlantic Coast Conference Scoreboard Conference Members Atlantic Coast Conference Main Schedule Main Sport Navigation Menu Men's Basketball March 10, 2026 Associated Press Boopie Miller Scores 25 and No. 11 Seed SMU Beats 14th-seeded Syracuse 86-69 in the ACC Tournament Story Links Highlights Box Score Box Score (PDF) SMU Quotes Syracuse Quotes Bracket (PDF) CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Boopie Miller scored 25 points to lead five starters in double figures, while Jaden Toombs and Samet Yigitoglu each had a double-double as No. 11 seed SMU beat 14th-seeded Syracuse 86-69 on Tuesday in the opening round of the 2026 T. Rowe Price ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament. SMU (20-12), which had lost four ACC games in a row, advanced to play 24th-ranked and sixth-seeded Louisville on Wednesday at 2:30 p.m. ET in the second round. Toombs had a double-double in the first half with 14 points and 10 rebounds, and Miller added 11 points to help SMU hold a 39-38 lead at the break. Nate Kingz paced Syracuse with 17 points. SMU pulled away midway through the second half when Jaron Pierre Jr. made three straight 3-pointers during a 13-0 run that ended in a 69-48 lead with 8:41 remaining. Pierre’s fifth 3-pointer of the second half made it 79-66. Pierre finished with 21 points for SMU. Toombs had 16 points, 11 rebounds and six assists; Yigitoglu had 12 points and 11 boards, and Corey Washington also scored 12. The Mustang reserves only attempted two shots. Kingz led Syracuse (15-17) with 25 points and Tyler Betsey added 15. The Orange shot 9-of-34 from distance (26%). Of Note SMU notched its second ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament victory, after also defeating the Orange in 2025. The Mustangs improve to 2-1 all-time in the ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament with an identical record in Charlotte. The Orange fall to 2-3 all-time in tournament games in Charlotte and 6-11 all-time in the ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament. Five SMU players scored in double figures, marking the 88th time in ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament history in which a team has had five double-figure scorers. The Mustangs got a pair of double-doubles from Samet Yigitoglu and Jaden Toombs. Toombs led the duo with 16 points, while each player snagged 11 rebounds. SMU did not record a single point off the bench on Tuesday, marking the first time in the ACC Men’s Basketball that a team did not tally a bench point since March 14, 2024, when both Duke and Wake Forest did so on the same day. The Mustangs made 11 3-pointers in the win, led by five apiece from Boopie Miller and Jaron Pierre Jr. Miller and Syracuse’s Nate Kingz shared the game-high in scoring with 25 points each. Syracuse’s Naithan George dished out 11 assists for the Orange – the most in a tournament loss since Virginia’s Reece Beekman in 2024. Related Stories and Videos Related

Roster attrition is a factor during the postseason in any sport. Optimistically, college basketball’s shorter regular season should mean the title picture is less altered by injuries than, say, the NBA. That is not necessarily the case this season, though. The injury bug has recently bitten two No. 1 seeds (Duke and Michigan), and a third (Arizona) played through a flurry of nagging injuries late in the season. Creation Date: 10/03/2019 --> Boopie Miller Scores 25 and No. 11 Seed SMU Beats 14th-seeded Syracuse 86-69 in the ACC Tournament - Atlantic Coast Conference Scoreboard Conference Members Atlantic Coast Conference Main Schedule Main Sport Navigation Menu Men's Basketball March 10, 2026 Associated Press Boopie Miller Scores 25 and No. 11 Seed SMU Beats 14th-seeded Syracuse 86-69 in the ACC Tournament Story Links Highlights Box Score Box Score (PDF) SMU Quotes Syracuse Quotes Bracket (PDF) CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Boopie Miller scored 25 points to lead five starters in double figures, while Jaden Toombs and Samet Yigitoglu each had a double-double as No. 11 seed SMU beat 14th-seeded Syracuse 86-69 on Tuesday in the opening round of the 2026 T. Rowe Price ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament. SMU (20-12), which had lost four ACC games in a row, advanced to play 24th-ranked and sixth-seeded Louisville on Wednesday at 2:30 p.m. ET in the second round. Toombs had a double-double in the first half with 14 points and 10 rebounds, and Miller added 11 points to help SMU hold a 39-38 lead at the break. Nate Kingz paced Syracuse with 17 points. SMU pulled away midway through the second half when Jaron Pierre Jr. made three straight 3-pointers during a 13-0 run that ended in a 69-48 lead with 8:41 remaining. Pierre’s fifth 3-pointer of the second half made it 79-66. Pierre finished with 21 points for SMU. Toombs had 16 points, 11 rebounds and six assists; Yigitoglu had 12 points and 11 boards, and Corey Washington also scored 12. The Mustang reserves only attempted two shots. Kingz led Syracuse (15-17) with 25 points and Tyler Betsey added 15. The Orange shot 9-of-34 from distance (26%). Of Note SMU notched its second ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament victory, after also defeating the Orange in 2025. The Mustangs improve to 2-1 all-time in the ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament with an identical record in Charlotte. The Orange fall to 2-3 all-time in tournament games in Charlotte and 6-11 all-time in the ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament. Five SMU players scored in double figures, marking the 88th time in ACC Men’s Basketball Tournament history in which a team has had five double-figure scorers. The Mustangs got a pair of double-doubles from Samet Yigitoglu and Jaden Toombs. Toombs led the duo with 16 points, while each player snagged 11 rebounds. SMU did not record a single point off the bench on Tuesday, marking the first time in the ACC Men’s Basketball that a team did not tally a bench point since March 14, 2024, when both Duke and Wake Forest did so on the same day. The Mustangs made 11 3-pointers in the win, led by five apiece from Boopie Miller and Jaron Pierre Jr. Miller and Syracuse’s Nate Kingz shared the game-high in scoring with 25 points each. Syracuse’s Naithan George dished out 11 assists for the Orange – the most in a tournament loss since Virginia’s Reece Beekman in 2024. Related Stories and Videos Related
